summary: Early‑'70 set shines with first FOTD and epic Dancin’ jam
blurb: The late March ’70 Capitol Theater show delivers a strong mix of new folk material and classic blues covers, highlighted by the debut of "Friend Of The Devil" and an 18‑minute "Dancin' In The Street" jam. Jerry’s melodic lead on "Easy Wind" and Pigpen’s percussive flavor add extra sparkle. Overall it’s a lively performance worth revisiting.
review: Port Chester’s Capitol Theater captured the Grateful Dead at a pivotal moment on March 21, 1970. This late‑show rolls through an eclectic setlist that swings from the raucous energy of "Casey Jones" and "Wake Up Little Susie" to the tender folk‑rock of the brand‑new "Friend Of The Devil." Highlights abound: a teasing "Nearer to the Fire" with a China Cat riff, a soaring 18‑minute "Dancin' In The Street" that showcases Jerry’s lyrical phrasing and Phil’s inventive bass, and a blistering “Easy Wind” solo that many listeners call perfect. Pigpen’s percussive embellishments on tracks like "Cosmic Charlie" add an extra layer of fun, while the classic “Saint Stephen > Not Fade Away” segue keeps the momentum rolling into a soulful “Turn On Your Lovelight.” Though the tape has some cuts and pitch quirks, the overall vibe is loose, spontaneous, and undeniably enjoyable – a snapshot of the Dead honing their early‑’70 sound.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising standout performances (first FOTD, long Dancin' jam, strong Easy Wind and Cosmic Charlie) with no consistent negative comments. The consensus across sources yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Solid vintage audience tape with good sound, captures the NFA/China Cat tease and classic covers; despite minor track duplication it offers the most complete listening experience.

key_highlights: First known performance of "Friend Of The Devil"
"Dancin' In The Street" 18‑minute jam showcases peak improvisation
NFA with a playful China Cat tease adds unique flavor
Pigpen’s percussion enriches the set, especially on "Cosmic Charlie"

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers melodic leads on "Easy Wind" and drives the long jams with his signature phrasing, even adding a playful touch during the Midnight Hour solo.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a creative, peak‑era bass line throughout the Dancin' jam, anchoring the improvisations with fluid movement.
band_performance.Bob: Offers an outstanding guitar solo within the Dancin' improvisation, adding excitement to the extended jam.
